ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'toast (food)' class. No specific multigrain thick toast with butter and sugar entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products. No specific multigrain thick toast sub-code exists; closest_parent used.

serving.description: 1 serving (2 slices thick toast with butter and sugar)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of two thick slices of multigrain toast spread with butter and sugar, weighing approximately 120 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, multigrain, toasted — adapted with butter and sugar spread components; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 18069 (multigrain bread) and item 01001 (butter) and item 19335 (granulated sugar)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Multigrain Butter Sugar Thick Toast exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for multigrain bread (toasted), salted butter, and granulated sugar in proportions consistent with a typical Singaporean thick-toast preparation (approximately 70 g multigrain bread, 15 g butter, 10 g sugar per 120 g serving, scaled to per-100 g basis). Energy cross-checked against HPB Singapore Foodo generic kaya toast entries for plausibility.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — multigrain bread GI reference; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Multigrain Butter Sugar Thick Toast as a composite dish. GI of 65 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for multigrain bread (GI ~65, medium category). The addition of butter (fat) would typically lower the effective GI slightly; however, the added sugar partially offsets this. A composite GI of 65 is retained as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per 120 g serving calculated as: (65 × 60.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 39.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from salted butter and bread
hypertension.guidance: Each 120 g serving provides approximately 372 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one serving and choose unsalted butter variants where available to reduce sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined sugar content and medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of added sugar (approximately 16.8 g per serving) and a medium GI of 65 produces a high glycemic load of approximately 39 per serving, making this food unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without portion control and blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and trans fat from butter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contributes approximately 8.9 g saturated fat and 0.4 g trans fat from butter, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and consider plant-based spread alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Multigrain Butter Sugar Thick Toast is a popular Singaporean breakfast and snack item served at traditional kopi (coffee shop) chains such as Toast Box. It consists of two thick slices of multigrain bread — made from a blend of whole grains including wheat, oats, and seeds — that are toasted until golden and crisp on the outside while remaining soft inside. The toast is generously spread with salted butter and topped with a layer of granulated white sugar, creating a sweet, rich, and slightly salty flavour profile. The texture contrasts a crunchy exterior with a pillowy interior, and the butter melts into the warm bread to produce a moist, indulgent mouthfeel. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 340 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content of 7.2 g, significant fat at 12.5 g (predominantly saturated from butter), and a high carbohydrate load of 50 g per 100 g including 14 g of sugar. The multigrain base contributes dietary fibre (3.2 g per 100 g) and B vitamins. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), yielding a high glycemic load per serving. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar and glycemic load, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated and trans fat content from butter. It is typically consumed as part of a traditional Singaporean breakfast set alongside soft-boiled eggs and kopi (local coffee).

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Multigrain Butter Sugar Thick Toast exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for multigrain bread (toasted), salted butter, and granulated sugar in proportions consistent with a typical Singaporean thick-toast preparation. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (multigrain bread) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy components (multigrain bread toasted, salted butter, granulated sugar) scaled to a per-100 g composite basis. No exact branded product entry was available in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for Toast Box multigrain thick toast. Vitamin C is 0.0 — confirmed zero as multigrain bread, butter, and sugar contain negligible vitamin C; this is naturally zero for this food combination. All -1 sentinel values indicate nutrients not reported or not reliably derivable from the component-based estimation approach used: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are populated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are therefore included as numeric estimates rather than -1. GI of 65 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database multigrain bread reference; glycemic load per 120 g serving = (65 × 60.0) / 100 = 39.0. Halal status assumed based on typical Toast Box ingredient sourcing; kosher status not confirmed. Follow-up: seek HPB Singapore Foodo or direct laboratory analysis for exact nutrient verification.
